concepts.name: Probability
concepts.short_definition: The measure of the likelihood of an event occurring.

concepts.name: Events
concepts.short_definition: A specific result or set of results of a random experiment.

concepts.name: Sample Space
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible outcomes of a random experiment.

concepts.name: Conditional Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of an event occurring given that another event has already occurred.

concepts.name: Joint Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of two or more events occurring simultaneously.

concepts.name: Marginal Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of a single event occurring, without considering other events.

concepts.name: Independence
concepts.short_definition: When the occurrence of one event does not affect the probability of another event.

concepts.name: Dependence
concepts.short_definition: When the occurrence of one event affects the probability of another event.

concepts.name: Bayes' Theorem
concepts.short_definition: A formula that describes the probability of an event based on prior knowledge of conditions that might be related to the event.

concepts.name: Conditional Probability Table
concepts.short_definition: A table used to display the frequency distribution of variables, often used to calculate conditional probabilities.
